channel.title: #DigitalBlackOutIran
channel.description: Daily simulation of how much GDP the world and the top 10 economies would lose if their own internet were blacked out for as many days as Iran's.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 108
item.description: Internet blackout day 108: modeled daily loss reaches $79.95B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 107
item.description: 2026-06-14 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $79.60B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 106
item.description: Day 106 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $79.24B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 105
item.description: 2026-06-12 internet blackout simulation: day 105, world scenario $78.89B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 104
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 104: world daily loss $78.54B, cumulative world loss $6.03T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 103
item.description: 2026-06-10 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $78.18B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 102
item.description: Day 102 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $12.22B/day, world $77.83B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 101
item.description: SEO update for 2026-06-08: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $77.48B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 100
item.description: Internet blackout day 100: modeled daily loss reaches $77.12B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 99
item.description: 2026-06-06 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $76.76B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 98
item.description: Day 98 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $76.41B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 97
item.description: 2026-06-04 internet blackout simulation: day 97, world scenario $76.05B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 96
item.description: Internet outage simulation day 96: world daily loss $75.69B, cumulative world loss $5.41T; United States remains the largest economy case.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 95
item.description: 2026-06-02 blackout simulation archive: United States, China and Germany top the loss table; world scenario totals $75.34B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 94
item.description: Day 94 blackout simulation report: United States $20.13B/day, China $11.77B/day, world $74.98B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 93
item.description: SEO update for 2026-05-31: simulated blackout losses show United States at $20.13B/day and world exposure at $74.62B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 92
item.description: Internet blackout day 92: modeled daily loss reaches $74.26B worldwide, with United States and China leading the country ranking.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 91
item.description: 2026-05-29 update: simulated internet blackout losses put United States first, China second, with world exposure at $73.90B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 90
item.description: Day 90 economic impact simulation: if internet went dark locally, United States would lose $20.13B/day; world $73.54B/day.

item.title: Internet blackout simulation update — day 89
item.description: 2026-05-27 internet blackout simulation: day 89, world scenario $73.18B/day. United States leads at $20.13B/day.
